title: Fix _uses_planner_owned_followups suppression when no shared followups exist
description:
Bug fix: _uses_planner_owned_followups returns True for any developer bead whose parent is a planner bead, even when no actual planner-owned shared followup beads exist. This causes the auto-followup suppression logic (lines 830, 851, 872 in scheduler.py) to suppress all followup creation, breaking test_followups_and_discovered_subtasks_inherit_feature_root (3 instances fail with 'Bead not found: B-xxx-review').

Root cause: In _uses_planner_owned_followups(), returning True purely based on parent agent_type='planner' is too broad. The suppression should only activate when planner-owned shared followup beads actually exist for this developer bead.

Fix: After computing planner_owned_followups, refine the uses_planner_owned flag to False if no planner-owned followup was actually found. Specifically, in the _create_followup_beads method (around line 797), after calling _planner_owned_followups_for(), add:
  uses_planner_owned = uses_planner_owned and any(planner_owned_followups.values())

This ensures:
- Developer beads with a planner parent but no shared followups → uses_planner_owned=False → legacy per-developer followup creation proceeds
- Developer beads with a planner parent AND actual shared followup beads → uses_planner_owned=True → suppression applies as designed

File to modify: src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py (in the B-8eb3f6c0 worktree at .orchestrator/worktrees/B-8eb3f6c0/).

After the fix, all 560 tests must pass. The key failing test is test_followups_and_discovered_subtasks_inherit_feature_root (in OrchestratorTests, DeleteBeadTests, DeleteBeadCliTests at line 2483 in tests/test_orchestrator.py).
acceptance_criteria: test_followups_and_discovered_subtasks_inherit_feature_root passes in all 3 test class instances
All 560 tests pass (uv run python -m unittest discover -s tests -v)
Existing coalesced followup tests still pass (test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_* etc.)

handoff_summary.completed: Added `uses_planner_owned = uses_planner_owned and any(planner_owned_followups.values())` after computing planner_owned_followups in _create_followup_beads (line ~802 in scheduler.py). This ensures suppression only activates when planner-owned shared followup beads actually exist.
handoff_summary.remaining: Tester agent should run all 560 tests to confirm test_followups_and_discovered_subtasks_inherit_feature_root passes in all 3 instances and no regressions in planner-owned followup tests.
handoff_summary.risks: Minimal. The change narrows the suppression condition; it cannot suppress more than before, only less. Existing planner-owned shared followup tests should be unaffected because they have actual planner-owned beads present.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run full test suite to validate fix
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only scheduler.py modified, single focused line addition.

conflict_risks: None — only scheduler.py modified, single focused line addition.

execution_history.summary: Fixed _uses_planner_owned_followups suppression in scheduler.py by adding a refinement line after _planner_owned_followups_for() is called. If no planner-owned followup beads actually exist, uses_planner_owned is reset to False, allowing the legacy per-developer followup creation path to proceed.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
